action.content:
Expand the riddle into a story with a funny tone:

What can you catch but not throw?
A cold

Use * to highlight at least 2 sections in your text. For example: *this is a highlighted text section*.
action.label: context

action.content:
Barnaby "The Cannon" McGee was a man who took the concept of "throwing" very seriously. Whether it was a football, a tantrum, or a half-eaten sandwich at a stray cat, Barnaby believed that if he could hold it, he could launch it with the force of a small rocket. One crisp Tuesday, he decided to enter the annual "Ultimate Catch-and-Throw" competition, confident that his biceps were ready to conquer any object the universe could conjure.

The first round was easy. A tennis ball? *Barnaby caught it with one hand and immediately threw it so hard it shattered a window three blocks away.* The crowd cheered, and the judges nodded in approval. The second round involved a heavy medicine ball. Barnaby grinned, caught it, and hurled it into the stratosphere. He was on a roll, a veritable god of projectile motion.

Then came the final round. The announcer, a nervous man named Steve, stepped up to the microphone. "For the grand finale," Steve squeaked, "we have a very special, invisible opponent. It is highly contagious, spreads easily in crowds, and once you have it, you cannot get rid of it by tossing it into the trash."

Barnaby puffed out his chest. "Bring it on! I'll catch it and throw it into the sun!"

Suddenly, a sneeze erupted from the front row. Then another. Within seconds, a cloud of microscopic germs descended upon the arena. Barnaby, being a man of action, instinctively reached out his hands. *He successfully caught a massive, shivering cold right in the palm of his hand.*

The crowd gasped. "Now, throw it!" someone shouted.

Barnaby squeezed his eyes shut, wound up his arm, and threw with all his might. But the cold didn't fly. It didn't bounce. It just... stayed. In fact, it seemed to multiply. Barnaby tried to throw it again, but his arm felt heavy, his nose began to run like a firehose, and his voice turned into a gravelly croak. He tried to toss it to the referee, but the referee just sneezed and caught it too.

"I can't throw it!" Barnaby wheezed, collapsing onto the podium. "It's stuck! It's like a boomerang that refuses to come back!"

The judges looked at each other, then at the rulebook. "Well," the head judge said, wiping a tear from his own eye, "technically, you did catch it. But the rule was 'catch and throw.' Since you can't throw a cold, you've lost the competition."

Barnaby lay there, wrapped in a blanket, surrounded by a dozen other contestants who had also caught the cold. "I guess," he sniffled miserably, "some things are just meant to be kept."
action.label: response
